item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In containment procedures you say people exposed to the posters should be given amnestics after 48 hours. That does not leave a lot of time for testing because derealization occurs gradually over 12-18 months. Were you talking about normal people outside the foundation who were exposed?

item.title: Licensing JS Post
item.content:encoded: Attempted contact with the creator of the image used on this article has been unsuccessful, thus the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am unable to believe that a physician would not only write things that are "outside of hospital code" and sign them, leaving open the possibility of those words being used against them, but also that they would do so on facility letterhead.

This is intentional, it's absurd a genuine doctor would write this. I go towards the explanation of the man slowly and unknowingly affecting this doctor as his abilities started to manifest, making him out to be the caricature that he is in his mind.

The concept is about a man with an extreme denial of reality that it becomes anomalous, and starts affecting the world around him. The opposite of a psychiatrist wanting to help and encourage recovery would be a resentful fed up doctor that believes what the patient thinks of himself. In this case, "I'm a hopeless case."

The letter is, in a way, the character talking about himself. If that makes sense lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I generally like the concept here and feel like something really fun could be built on it, but I have two specific issues that have caused me to give this a -1. One issue is that the dialog doesn't really read to me the way people would naturally speak to each other and comes off as very script-y - I think if you give it a read out loud you may feel the same way. The other is that, as a person who deals with medical correspondence daily, I am unable to believe that a physician would not only write things that are "outside of hospital code" and sign them, leaving open the possibility of those words being used against them, but also that they would do so on facility letterhead. Fixing those two would go a long way towards turning my opinion to the positive, but I am only one person with one person's opinions so take or leave them as you like. Thanks for sharing your writing with us!

item.title: Admin + Technical Captain Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that I've removed the offsite hosted CSS which was in violation of the css policy.

Additionally, removing the translation block is directly against the CSS policy, unless you provide for all translations in your author post, which you have not done.

Do not re-add these without rectifying your errors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you get explicit permission from the Twitter user to use their picture in this article?

item.title: Re: One little problem
item.content:encoded:
Oh yeah, you're right. I'll shorten the footnote and see if that fixes it.

Edit: It did :)

item.title: One little problem
item.content:encoded:
Ok, this is really well written.
There is one thing that needs fixing though, and that’s footnote 1. Derealization clips out of the box on mobile. I don’t really have a opinion on it yet, so no vote.
